resume:
Gregory Okafor
Senior Supply Chain Manager — Global Planning & Procurement
Chicago, IL | g.okafor@example.com | linkedin.com/in/gokafor

PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Seasoned senior supply chain leader with twelve years of progressive experience spanning demand planning, procurement, supplier management, logistics, and S&OP across consumer-goods and industrial-manufacturing environments. I bring a relentless bias for action and a passion for building world-class, resilient, customer-obsessed supply chains that delight stakeholders end to end. I thrive at the intersection of strategy and execution, partnering cross-functionally to unlock value, drive operational excellence, and deliver against ambitious north-star objectives. I am a servant leader who builds high-performing teams, fosters a culture of continuous improvement, and leans into ambiguity to turn complex challenges into scalable, future-proof outcomes. I am deeply fluent in lean principles, Six Sigma methodologies, and digital-supply-chain transformation, and I am energized by the opportunity to operationalize next-generation planning capabilities at enterprise scale.

CORE COMPETENCIES
Demand Planning · Supply Planning · S&OP / IBP · Strategic Sourcing · Supplier Relationship Management · Inventory Optimization · Logistics & Distribution · Network Design · Procurement Strategy · Cost Reduction · Risk Mitigation · Lean / Six Sigma · ERP (SAP) · Advanced Planning Systems · Stakeholder Management · Cross-Functional Leadership · Change Management · KPI Governance · Continuous Improvement

PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

Senior Supply Chain Manager — Brightline Consumer Brands (2020–present)
Lead a cross-functional planning and procurement organization supporting a multi-category consumer-goods portfolio across North American distribution. Own the end-to-end S&OP process, partner with commercial and finance leadership to balance service and cost, and steward a portfolio of strategic supplier relationships. I have championed a culture of data-driven decision-making and operational rigor, driving meaningful improvements in service levels, inventory health, and total landed cost. I orchestrated a digital-planning transformation initiative, partnering with IT and external vendors to modernize our planning toolset and unlock advanced analytics capabilities. I have also led supplier-consolidation and cost-optimization efforts, negotiating with strategic partners to deliver favorable commercial terms while de-risking the supply base. I mentor a team of planners and buyers and have built a robust talent pipeline and succession plan. I serve as the connective tissue between commercial ambition and operational reality, translating top-line growth aspirations into executable supply plans and ensuring our network is positioned to flex with demand volatility. I lead the monthly executive S&OP review, synthesize the demand and supply consensus into a single integrated plan, and surface the risks and opportunities that matter most to the leadership team. I have institutionalized a cadence of weekly exception management, monthly portfolio reviews, and quarterly network assessments, and I have embedded continuous-improvement rituals so that every planner is empowered to identify and eliminate waste. I partner closely with our finance business partners to ensure our plans are financially grounded and that we are making the right tradeoffs between working-capital efficiency and customer service. I am a vocal advocate for resilience and have led scenario-planning exercises to stress-test our network against disruption.

Supply Chain Manager — Continental Industrial Supply (2016–2020)
Managed demand and supply planning for an industrial-components business unit. Drove S&OP maturity, partnered with sourcing on supplier performance, and led inventory-optimization initiatives across multiple distribution centers. Spearheaded a SKU-rationalization program and collaborated with logistics on network-efficiency improvements. Recognized for fostering strong cross-functional alignment and for cultivating a continuous-improvement mindset across the planning organization. I built the demand-planning playbook from the ground up, introduced a structured forecast-accuracy governance process, and partnered with commercial to drive a more disciplined consensus-forecasting motion. I championed the adoption of advanced statistical forecasting methods and worked hand in hand with our sales and marketing partners to incorporate market intelligence into the baseline. I also led a comprehensive inventory-segmentation effort, aligning safety-stock policy to service-criticality and demand variability so that we could intelligently right-size inventory across the portfolio while protecting service on our most critical items.

Procurement Lead — Halverson Manufacturing (2013–2016)
Led strategic sourcing and procurement for direct materials. Managed supplier negotiations, drove cost-savings initiatives, and implemented supplier-scorecarding to elevate quality and delivery performance. Partnered with engineering and operations to align sourcing strategy with production requirements. I owned category strategy across several direct-material commodities, ran structured RFP and negotiation processes, and built durable supplier partnerships grounded in mutual accountability and shared performance goals. I also stood up a supplier-development program to elevate the capability and reliability of our strategic partners.

Supply Chain Analyst — Tarrytown Logistics (2011–2013)
Supported demand forecasting, inventory analysis, and logistics reporting. Built dashboards and supported S&OP cadence. Partnered with planners and operations to translate raw data into actionable insight, and supported the monthly planning rhythm with rigorous analysis and clear reporting.

EDUCATION & CERTIFICATIONS
M.B.A., Operations & Supply Chain Management — Northwestern University (Kellogg)
B.S., Industrial Engineering — University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
APICS CPIM · Lean Six Sigma Green Belt

LEADERSHIP PHILOSOPHY
I believe great supply chains are built on great people, disciplined processes, and a relentless commitment to the customer. I lead by empowering my teams, removing barriers, and creating clarity amid complexity. I am passionate about building resilient, agile, and future-ready supply chains that serve as a true competitive advantage and a source of enduring value creation for the enterprise and its stakeholders.

ADDITIONAL
Frequent contributor to internal operational-excellence forums; mentor in the company's emerging-leaders program; fluent in English and conversational French.
jd: Brightline Consumer Brands is hiring a Senior Supply Chain Manager to own end-to-end planning and procurement for a multi-category consumer-goods portfolio. You will run the S&OP/IBP process, own service-level and inventory targets, and lead strategic-sourcing and cost-optimization programs with our supplier base. We need a leader who can be specific and quantitative about the decisions THEY drove: the actual service-level or inventory-turn numbers, the sourcing tradeoffs they made, and how they measured impact. Buzzword-heavy narratives about 'operational excellence' and 'world-class supply chains' without hard specifics will not pass our bar. You will partner with commercial, finance, and logistics, and present S&OP outcomes to executive leadership. Strong candidates speak in concrete mechanisms, numbers, and named tradeoffs.

history.q: Give me the concrete shape of your role at Brightline — what exactly do you own in the S&OP process and what are the service and inventory targets?
Tell me about the supplier-consolidation and cost-optimization work. What specifically did you do and what tradeoff did you have to make?

candidate_last_answer: Absolutely — supplier consolidation is one of those areas where I really lean into operational excellence and a total-value mindset. What we did was take a very strategic, end-to-end view of the supply base, rationalize toward our highest-performing partners, and drive a step-change in commercial terms while de-risking the network. It was all about unlocking value, building resilience, and creating a win-win partnership model. I partnered cross-functionally with sourcing, finance, and quality to align on a north-star, and we executed with real discipline and rigor. The result was a more agile, future-ready supply base and meaningful, sustainable savings that delighted our stakeholders. I'm a big believer that you optimize the whole system, not the parts, so we were very thoughtful about balancing cost, risk, and service holistically. Net-net, it was a transformational outcome that positioned us as a true competitive advantage.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: a step-change in commercial terms while de-risking the network

expected-yield: a single concrete consolidation decision with the real tradeoff named — how many suppliers were cut to how many, the dual-source vs. single-source risk he accepted on a specific category, the savings percentage or dollar figure, and what service or continuity exposure that created and how he hedged it
